name: Archon1
description: First genetically encoded voltage indicator derived from archaerhodopsin-3 (Arch3), enabling direct imaging of membrane potential with millisecond temporal resolution in neurons.

researchPapers.title: A robotic multidimensional directed evolution approach applied to fluorescent voltage reporters
researchPapers.authors: Piatkevich E, et al.
researchPapers.sample: Cultured neurons, Brain slices, Zebrafish, C. elegans

name: Archon2
description: An improved version of Archon1 with enhanced plasma membrane localization and reduced intracellular aggregation, enabling more reliable neuronal imaging.

name: Archon3
description: Third-generation Archon sensor with improved targeting and mutations that enhance fluorescence while maintaining fast rhodopsin kinetics for neural imaging.

name: QuasAr1
description: First quantum dot-like microbial rhodopsin voltage indicator requiring no exogenous fluorophore, enabling fluorescence detection with minimal illumination.

name: QuasAr2
description: Improved QuasAr variant with brighter fluorescence and better expression in neurons while maintaining ultrafast response to voltage changes.

name: paQuasAr3
description: Third-generation QuasAr with orange/far-red shift for improved tissue penetration and reduced phototoxicity in deep imaging applications.

name: ASAP1
description: First generation ArcLight-based ASAP sensor using cpGFP fused to voltage-sensing domain, providing bright fluorescence that decreases with depolarization.

name: ASAP2s
description: Optimized ASAP variant with improved plasma membrane targeting and increased dynamic range through mutations in the voltage-sensing domain.

name: ASAP3
description: High-performance ASAP sensor with 51% fluorescence modulation, submillisecond kinetics, and improved signal-to-noise ratio for reliable spike detection.

researchPapers.title: Ultrafast Two-Photon Imaging of a High-Gain Voltage Indicator in Awake Behaving Mice
researchPapers.authors: Piatkevich E, et al.
researchPapers.sample: Cultured neurons, Brain slices, In vivo mice

name: ASAP4b
description: ASAP4b is the bright variant of the ASAP4 family with exceptional dynamic range (≥180% ΔF/F per 100mV), producing positive-going fluorescence increase upon depolarization. Designed for one- and two-photon imaging with high photostability.

kinetics.notes: Time from crossing 20mV to fluorescence peak in Kv variant

applicationNotes: ASAP4b-Kv shows significantly higher SNR than ASAP3-Kv in two-photon imaging. Enables single-trial detection of spikes in mice.

researchPapers.title: Positive-going hybrid indicators for voltage imaging in excitable cells and tissues
researchPapers.authors: Kannan M, Vaidya V, Miao J, Huang K, Chavez J, Chang K, Hariraam J, Roopa BL, Nagarajan S, Ananth A, Liu H, Zhou Y, Song Y, Xu C
researchPapers.sample: Cultured neurons, mouse brain cortex, acute brain slices

researchPapers.title: Voltage imaging reveals hippocampal inhibitory dynamics shaping pyramidal memory-encoding sequences
researchPapers.authors: Ratz M, Bewersdorf J, Younts T, Kuhlman SJ

researchPapers.title: Imaging sensory transmission and neuronal plasticity in primary sensory neurons with a positively tuned voltage indicator
researchPapers.authors: Chaves G, Taha E, Abdallah C, Karapetyan G, Zgraggen E, Gouty S, Huang K, Kannan M, Kuhlman S, Sumbre G

name: ASAP4e
description: ASAP4e is the enhanced/speed variant of the ASAP4 family with fast kinetics while maintaining high dynamic range (≥180% ΔF/F per 100mV). Produces positive-going fluorescence increase upon depolarization. Optimized for fast imaging applications.

kinetics.notes: Time from crossing 20mV to fluorescence peak in Kv variant

applicationNotes: ASAP4e enables single-trial detection of spikes in mice over the course of minutes under standard microscopy equipment. Performs well under two-photon illumination.

researchPapers.title: Positive-going hybrid indicators for voltage imaging in excitable cells and tissues
researchPapers.authors: Kannan M, Vaidya V, Miao J, Huang K, Chavez J, Chang K, Hariraam J, Roopa BL, Nagarajan S, Ananth A, Liu H, Zhou Y, Song Y, Xu C
researchPapers.sample: Cultured neurons, mouse brain cortex, acute brain slices

researchPapers.title: Voltage imaging reveals hippocampal inhibitory dynamics shaping pyramidal memory-encoding sequences
researchPapers.authors: Ratz M, Bewersdorf J, Younts T, Kuhlman SJ

researchPapers.title: Imaging sensory transmission and neuronal plasticity in primary sensory neurons with a positively tuned voltage indicator
researchPapers.authors: Chaves G, Taha E, Abdallah C, Karapetyan G, Zgraggen E, Gouty S, Huang K, Kannan M, Kuhlman S, Sumbre G

name: ArcLight
description: Classic genetically encoded voltage indicator using a voltage-sensing domain fused to pH-sensitive GFP, producing large fluorescence decreases during depolarization.

researchPapers.title: Genetically expressed voltage sensor ArcLight for imaging large-scale neuronal activity
researchPapers.authors: Cao H, et al.

name: ArcLight-D
description: Improved ArcLight variant with mutations that enhance plasma membrane targeting and improve signal amplitude in neuronal processes.

name: Voltron
description: Breakthrough chemigenetic voltage indicator using HaloTag-fluorophore conjugates, providing 10x brighter signal than previous GEVIs for in vivo imaging.

name: HVI+
description: Hybrid Voltage Indicator+ (HVI+) is a positive-going chemigenetic voltage indicator with 55% ΔF/F0 per 100mV, fast kinetics (rise ~2.2ms, decay ~1.3ms), and excellent photostability (half-life ~736s).

name: Voltron2
description: Second-generation Voltron with improved fluorophore options and better photostability, enabling extended imaging sessions in behaving animals.

researchPapers.title: Sensitivity optimization of a rhodopsin-based fluorescent voltage indicator
researchPapers.authors: Abdelfattah AS, et al.
researchPapers.sample: Cultured neurons, Acute brain slices, In vivo mice, Zebrafish, Drosophila

researchPapers.title: Photophysics-informed two-photon voltage imaging using FRET-opsins
researchPapers.authors: Brooks F, et al.

researchPapers.title: Imaging neuronal voltage beyond the scattering limit
researchPapers.authors: Wang X, et al.

researchPapers.title: Large-scale deep tissue voltage imaging with targeted illumination
researchPapers.authors: Song C, et al.

researchPapers.title: Two-photon voltage imaging with rhodopsin-based sensors
researchPapers.authors: Evans SW, et al.

researchPapers.title: Orange/far-red hybrid voltage indicators with reduced phototoxicity
researchPapers.authors: Liu Y, et al.

name: Ace1
description: First voltage indicator based on bacterial channelrhodopsin with unprecedented speed around 1ms response, enabling high-fidelity spike detection.

name: Ace2N-mNeon
description: Ace2N-mNeon is an eFRET GEVI based on the Ace opsin channelrhodopsin fused to mNeon, enabling fast voltage imaging with improved membrane trafficking and targeting in mammalian neurons.

name: Ace2N-mNeon2
description: Ace2N-mNeon2 is an improved version of Ace2N-mNeon with enhanced brightness and faster kinetics, optimized for high-speed voltage imaging in neurons.

name: SomArchon
description: SomArchon enables population imaging in awake behaving mice, combining millisecond kinetics with compatibility for optogenetic manipulation.

name: VSFP1
description: The first genetically encoded voltage indicator, using GFP fused to voltage-sensing domain from Kv channels, pioneering the VSFP architecture.

name: VSFP2
description: Second-generation VSFP with improved CFP-YFP FRET pair, providing ratiometric voltage sensing for improved signal stability.

name: VSFP2.3
description: Optimized VSFP2 variant with improved plasma membrane targeting and faster response kinetics for imaging in C. elegans neurons.

name: chi-VSFP
description: VSFP variant engineered using cyclic hypothesis-improved design, enhancing voltage sensitivity through structural modifications.

name: VSFP-Butterfly
description: VSFP with butterfly motif design enabling dual-color ratiometric imaging for improved quantification of membrane potential.

name: FlicR1
description: Bright red fluorescent voltage indicator based on CiVSD-cpTrefoil architecture, providing reliable action potential detection in neurons.

name: Rho1
description: Next-generation rhodopsin voltage indicator engineered for improved brightness and reduced DNA toxicity while maintaining fast kinetics.

name: Bongwoori
description: Korean-developed VSFP variant with improved expression and signal amplitude in mammalian neurons.

name: NIR-GEV1
description: First near-infrared GEVI based on bacterial rhodopsin, enabling deeper tissue imaging with reduced scattering.

name: NIR-GEV2
description: Improved near-infrared GEVI with enhanced brightness and faster kinetics for in vivo deep tissue voltage imaging.

name: Electric
description: Recent microbial rhodopsin sensor engineered for improved photostability and subthreshold sensitivity in brain imaging.

name: Pado
description: Rhodopsin-based sensor developed through machine learning-guided engineering, balancing speed and fluorescence properties.

name: ProbeDB
description: FP-based sensor discovered through large-scale screening of genetically encoded voltage indicators, selected for improved performance.

name: synth
description: Synthetic biology approach combining multiple protein domains to engineer a novel GEVI architecture with optimized properties.

name: ASAP5
description: ASAP5 is a fast and responsive voltage indicator with enhanced sensitivity for unitary synaptic events. Features submillisecond kinetics at physiological temperature (0.78 ms activation at 37C) and high voltage sensitivity of -0.83%/mV, enabling detection of single synaptic events.

kinetics.notes: Activation and deactivation time constants at room temperature (23C) and physiological temperature (37C)

applicationNotes: Optimized for detecting unitary synaptic events (mEPSPs) in cultured neurons and in vivo imaging. Can match chemigenetic indicators (Voltron2) in SNR despite lower brightness due to higher responsivity.

name: JEDI-1P
description: JEDI (Java-encoded DNA indicator) family of GEVIs with improved brightness and dynamic range for in vivo imaging.

researchPapers.title: Widefield imaging of rapid pan-cortical voltage dynamics with an indicator evolved for one-photon microscopy

name: JEDI-2P
description: Two-photon optimized JEDI variant for deep tissue voltage imaging with improved photostability.

name: rEstus
description: Ultrasensitive GEVI optimized for detecting subthreshold voltage changes with high fidelity.

name: QuasAr6
description: Bright far-red QuasAr variant with improved quantum yield for better signal in deep tissue imaging.

name: VARNAM
description: Voltage indicator using opsin-FRET architecture with red fluorescent protein for improved tissue penetration.

name: Positron
description: Positive-going eFRET voltage indicator that increases fluorescence with depolarization, opposite to most opsin-based sensors.

name: PROPS
description: First proteorhodopsin-based voltage indicator, establishing the microbial opsin GEVI architecture.

name: Archer1
description: Archaerhodopsin-based voltage indicator with improved brightness over early versions.

name: MacQ
description: Macrolide-based eFRET voltage indicator combining opsin with fluorescent protein.

name: Mermaid
description: Early FRET-based voltage indicator using Ci-VSP voltage sensing domain with improved fluorescent protein pair.

name: VSFP-Butterfly
description: VSFP variant with improved membrane targeting and FRET efficiency for better voltage sensitivity.

name: nirButterfly
description: Near-infrared VSFP variant using NIR fluorescent proteins for improved tissue penetration.

name: LOTUS-V
description: Bioluminescent voltage indicator combining luminescent protein with voltage-sensing domain, eliminating excitation light.

name: AMBER
description: Latest bioluminescent GEVI with improved brightness and voltage sensitivity through luciferase-VSD fusion.

name: Solaris
description: Solaris is a panel of bright and sensitive hybrid voltage indicators with >2-fold higher voltage sensitivity than Voltron2. Solaris585 shows -60.6% ΔF/F₀ per 100mV and -28.1% per action potential, with response kinetics of τ₁/₂ = 1.75ms.

dynamicRangeData.notes: HEK293T cells; -28.1% per AP in neurons

researchPapers.title: Solaris: a panel of bright and sensitive hybrid voltage indicators for imaging membrane potential in cultured neurons
researchPapers.authors: Yang J, Zhu S, Yang L, Peng L, Han Y, Hayward RF, Park P, Hu D, Cohen AE, Zou P

name: Marina
description: Single FP voltage indicator derived from Ci-VSP with improved expression and targeting.
